## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References
146	
147	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
149	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
150	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
152	
153	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAg
154	
155	## Character
156	
157	Final assembly of the gauntlet test suite. Defines the suite as a composition of fixtures, creates the suite tabtarget, and pivots the rbw-tP workbench tabtarget from the originally-planned orchestrator-script shape to entry-contract enforcer + suite tabtarget exec. No new test cases; this pace is composition + entry point only.
158	
159	Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time.
160	
161	## Scope
162	
163	- Gauntlet suite definition: bash array in the engine holding the ordered fixture sequence per paddock's state ladder: `enrollment-validation` → `pristine-lifecycle` → `canonical-establish` → `canonical-onboarding-sequence` → `regime-validation` → `regime-smoke` → `four-mode` → `tadmor` → `moriah` → `srjcl` → `pluml`
164	- Resolver branch in the suite-name resolver
165	- New TestSuite tabtarget for the gauntlet suite
166	- Reshape rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh: enforce entry contract (marshal-zero signature, RBRR blank, RBRA absent, regimes blank) then exec the gauntlet suite tabtarget
167	
168	## Cross-pace dependencies
169	
170	- gauntlet-canonical-onboarding-sequence landed (final new fixture exists)
171	
172	## Out of scope
173	
174	- First-run end-to-end validation (BBAAX)
175	- Pristine-passed sentinel artifact (BBAAH)
176	- Documentation updates beyond what the workbench tabtarget needs (BBAAI)
177

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAg

## Character

Final assembly of the gauntlet test suite. Defines the suite as a composition of fixtures, creates the suite tabtarget, and pivots the rbw-tP workbench tabtarget from the originally-planned orchestrator-script shape to entry-contract enforcer + suite tabtarget exec. No new test cases; this pace is composition + entry point only.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time.

## Scope

- Gauntlet suite definition: bash array in the engine holding the ordered fixture sequence per paddock's state ladder: `enrollment-validation` → `pristine-lifecycle` → `canonical-establish` → `canonical-onboarding-sequence` → `regime-validation` → `regime-smoke` → `four-mode` → `tadmor` → `moriah` → `srjcl` → `pluml`
- Resolver branch in the suite-name resolver
- New TestSuite tabtarget for the gauntlet suite
- Reshape rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh: enforce entry contract (marshal-zero signature, RBRR blank, RBRA absent, regimes blank) then exec the gauntlet suite tabtarget

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-canonical-onboarding-sequence landed (final new fixture exists)

## Out of scope

- First-run end-to-end validation (BBAAX)
- Pristine-passed sentinel artifact (BBAAH)
- Documentation updates beyond what the workbench tabtarget needs (BBAAI)

message.content: Does this plan result in a test sequence that repeats cases or fixtures?

content.text:
No — within the gauntlet, every fixture appears exactly once, no case repeats.

The list has 11 distinct fixtures, each at a distinct state-ladder position. What might *look* like repetition is actually intentional staging:

- `enrollment-validation` runs as a **state-indifferent preflight** (position 1)
- `regime-validation` and `regime-smoke` run **after** canonical-establish + canonical-onboarding-sequence populate state (positions 5–6) — they verify populated regimes, which is different work than verifying empty regimes
- `tadmor`/`moriah`/`srjcl`/`pluml` consume vessels that `canonical-onboarding-sequence` already built (vessel-exists precondition per BBAAs), so no rebuild duplication

Cross-suite overlap exists but is tier-layered, not stacked: `rbw-tf` (fast) covers enrollment-validation + regime-validation + regime-smoke for cheap dev iteration; `rbw-tP` (gauntlet) re-runs them in their proper state-ladder positions during release. The paddock explicitly designs the operator to *not* run both back-to-back — `rbw-tr` is "pre-pristine smoke test," `rbw-tP` is the release gate.

One caveat worth surfacing at code-time: if any existing fixture's *cases* internally do work that's now also covered by pristine-lifecycle / canonical-establish / canonical-onboarding-sequence, that'd be redundant. I'll watch for that when reading the new fixtures' Rust modules — but the paddock's section labels (§1/§2/§3 + post-§3 regime checks + §4 per-crucible) suggest the decomposition was specifically designed to avoid it.

set -euo pipefail

RBTW_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

buc_context "${0##*/}"

# Source CLI (performs light furnish: buv + burd + buz + rbz + rbte kindle)
source "${RBTW_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbte_cli.sh"

rbtw_main() {
  local z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"

  rbte_dispatch "${z_command}" "$@"
}

rbtw_main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBTE Engine - Theurge test engine implementation module
#
# BCG module providing kindle/sentinel/public functions for theurge
# Rust build, test, and orchestration commands.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZRBTE_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBTE_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Kindle

zrbte_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbte already kindled"

  local z_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

  readonly RBTE_MANIFEST="${z_dir}/Cargo.toml"
  readonly ZRBTE_BINARY="${z_dir}/target/debug/rbtd"

  # Theurge-own colophons (routed by rbte_dispatch, not the RBK zipper)
  readonly ZRBTE_COLOPHONS="rbtd-ap"

  # Combined manifest: RBK zipper colophons + theurge-own colophons
  readonly ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST="${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST} ${ZRBTE_COLOPHONS}"

  # Suite-to-fixture mappings
  ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST=("enrollment-validation" "regime-validation" "regime-smoke" "handbook-render")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "four-mode" "batch-vouch")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "four-mode" "batch-vouch" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")

  readonly ZRBTE_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Sentinel

zrbte_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

zrbte_build_binary() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Building theurge"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
}

zrbte_resolve_suite() {
  local z_suite="$1"
  case "${z_suite}" in
    fast)     echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[*]}" ;;
    service)  echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE[*]}" ;;
    crucible) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE[*]}" ;;
    complete) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE[*]}" ;;
    *)        buc_die "Unknown suite: ${z_suite} (expected fast|service|crucible|complete)" ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Public functions

rbte_build() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Building theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  buc_success "Theurge built"
}

rbte_test() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Testing theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo test --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo test failed"
  buc_success "All theurge tests passed"
}

rbte_run() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
}

rbte_suite() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_suite="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_suite}" || buc_die "No suite imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh)"

  local z_fixture_list
  z_fixture_list="$(zrbte_resolve_suite "${z_suite}")"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_count=0
  local z_fixture
  for z_fixture in ${z_fixture_list}; do
    buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
    "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
  done

  buc_success "Suite '${z_suite}' complete (${z_count} fixtures)"
}

rbte_single() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-s.FixtureCase.tadmor.sh)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_case="${1:-}"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}" ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
}

rbte_probe() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local -r z_role="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_role}" || buc_die "No role imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.governor.sh)"

  case "${z_role}" in
    governor|director|retriever)
      # JWT SA propagation-absorbing budget: 60 attempts × 3000ms ≈ 180s worst case.
      # Happy path exits on first 2xx; budget consumed only when post-mint IAM
      # propagation lags. Each retry mints a fresh JWT-OAuth token, so the inner
      # rbgo_get_token_capture race (BBAAp) is exercised independently per attempt.
      local -r z_iterations=60
      local -r z_delay_ms=3000
      buc_step "JWT SA access probe: ${z_role}"
      rbgv_jwt_sa_probe "${z_role}" "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
      buc_success "${z_role} JWT access probe passed"
      ;;
    payor)
      # Payor probe semantic unchanged: stability-sample loop.
      local -r z_iterations=5
      local -r z_delay_ms=1500
      buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe"
      source "${RBBC_rbrp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRP: ${RBBC_rbrp_file}"
      zrbrp_kindle
      zrbrp_enforce
      rbgv_payor_oauth_probe "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
      buc_success "Payor OAuth access probe passed"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown access-probe role: ${z_role} (expected governor|director|retriever|payor)"
      ;;
  esac
}

# eof

content.text:
Architecture is **bash-orchestrated multiple theurge invocations** — not a single-process suite runner.

The flow:

```
tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh           (3-line tabtarget launcher)
  └→ .buk/launcher.rbtw_workbench.sh
      └→ rbtw_workbench.sh → rbte_dispatch → rbte_suite()   (bash, in rbte_engine.sh)
          └→ for z_fixture in $(zrbte_resolve_suite "$name); do
                "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${manifest}" "${z_fixture}"   (one Rust process per fixture)
             done
```

The Rust theurge binary (`rbtd/src/main.rs:49 run_suite`) takes exactly **one fixture**: verify manifest, charge, run all cases, quench, exit. Each fixture in a "TestSuite" is a separate Rust process — fresh charge/quench per fixture. The bash for-loop in `rbte_engine.sh:131` is the suite orchestrator.

Suite definitions are bash arrays in `zrbte_kindle` (rbte_engine.sh:48–51):
```
ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST=("enrollment-validation" "regime-validation" "regime-smoke" "handbook-render")
ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "four-mode" "batch-vouch")
ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE=(...)
```
Resolver is a case statement (`zrbte_resolve_suite`, line 74).

**Three subtle things this reveals for the pace:**

1. **Drift in the docket vs. existing kit.** The docket says "bash array in the engine," matching the existing pattern exactly — good. But it also says "Reshape rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh: enforce entry contract … then exec the gauntlet suite tabtarget." That part is correct: `rbw-tP` is a different launcher (`launcher.rbw_workbench.sh`, not `rbtw_`), so it stays a separate top-level tabtarget that bash-gates the entry contract and then `exec`s `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh`.

2. **Suite-level fail-fast is already free.** The bash for-loop runs under `set -euo pipefail` (rbte_engine.sh:24) — any rbtd nonzero exit kills the loop immediately. The paddock's "any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures" is already mechanically enforced; we don't need to add fail-fast logic.

3. **`handbook-render` is in `ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST` but absent from the paddock's gauntlet ladder.** Worth flagging: the paddock-listed gauntlet is enrollment-validation → pristine-lifecycle → canonical-establish → canonical-onboarding-sequence → regime-validation → regime-smoke → four-mode → tadmor → moriah → srjcl → pluml, with no `handbook-render`. Either the paddock predates that fixture's addition to fast, or the omission is intentional. Worth a check before commit.

So the pace is mechanically small: add one bash array (`ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET`), one resolver case branch (`gauntlet)`), one tabtarget (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh`), and rewrite `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh` body — though `rbw-tP` doesn't exist yet (only `rbw-tf` and `rbw-tr` are present in `tt/`), so it's a new tabtarget plus its rbw workbench routing.

# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zrbz_healthcheck() {

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBQ Qualify - Qualification orchestrator for tabtarget and colophon health

set -euo pipefail

ZRBQ_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buq_qualify.sh"

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBQ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbq multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBQ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbq_*)

zrbq_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBQ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbq already kindled"

  zbuz_sentinel
  zrbz_sentinel
  zrbcc_sentinel

  test -n "${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURC_TABTARGET_DIR not set"
  test -n "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}"     || buc_die "BURC_TOOLS_DIR not set"

  readonly ZRBQ_TT_DIR="${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}"
  readonly ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/.."
  readonly ZRBQ_RBW_DIR="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}"
  readonly ZRBQ_RBW_LAUNCHER=".buk/launcher.rbw_workbench.sh"

  readonly ZRBQ_KINDLED=1
}

zrbq_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBQ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbq not kindled - call zrbq_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbq_*)

rbq_qualify_colophons() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Qualifying RBW colophon registrations"

  local z_fail_files=()
  local z_fail_reasons=()
  local z_checked=0

  local z_file=""
  for z_file in "${ZRBQ_TT_DIR}"/*.sh; do
    test -e "${z_file}" || continue

    local z_basename="${z_file##*/}"

    # Load file lines (load-then-iterate)
    local z_lines=()
    local z_line=""
    while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
      z_lines+=("${z_line}")
    done < "${z_file}"

    # Only check tabtargets using the RBW workbench launcher
    local z_is_rbw=0
    if (( ${#z_lines[@]} > 1 )); then
      case "${z_lines[1]}" in
        *"${ZRBQ_RBW_LAUNCHER}"*) z_is_rbw=1 ;;
      esac
    fi
    test "${z_is_rbw}" = "1" || continue

    z_checked=$((z_checked + 1))

    # Extract colophon from filename (everything before first delimiter)
    local z_colophon="${z_basename%%.*}"

    # Check colophon exists in z_buz_colophon_roll registry
    local z_found=0
    local z_i=0
    for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
      test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
      z_found=1

      # Verify module file exists
      local z_module="${z_buz_module_roll[$z_i]}"
      test -f "${ZRBQ_RBW_DIR}/${z_module}" || {
        z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
        z_fail_reasons+=("module not found: ${z_module}")
      }
      break
    done

    test "${z_found}" = "1" || {
      z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
      z_fail_reasons+=("colophon '${z_colophon}' not registered in rbz_zipper")
    }
  done

  buc_log_args "Checked ${z_checked} RBW tabtargets"

  if (( ${#z_fail_files[@]} )); then
    local z_j=0
    for z_j in "${!z_fail_files[@]}"; do
      buc_warn "${z_fail_files[$z_j]}: ${z_fail_reasons[$z_j]}"
    done
    buc_die "Colophon qualification failed: ${#z_fail_files[@]} of ${z_checked} tabtargets"
  fi

  buc_log_args "All ${z_checked} RBW colophons registered"
}

rbq_qualify_context() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Qualifying generated context freshness"

  local -r z_committed="${ZRBQ_RBW_DIR}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"
  local -r z_fresh="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbq_context_check.md"

  buz_emit_context "${ZRBQ_TT_DIR}" > "${z_fresh}" || buc_die "Failed to generate context for comparison"

  if ! test -f "${z_committed}"; then
    buc_die "Generated context file missing: ${z_committed} — run tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh"
  fi

  if ! diff -q "${z_fresh}" "${z_committed}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_die "Generated context file is stale: ${z_committed} — run tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh"
  fi

  buc_log_args "Context file is fresh"
}

rbq_qualify_fast() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Running fast qualification"

  buq_tabtargets "${ZRBQ_TT_DIR}" "${ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT}" \
    "butctt.*.sh" \
    "buw-SI.*.sh" \
    # End of exempt list
  rbq_qualify_colophons
  rbq_qualify_context
  rbrn_preflight

  buc_step "Fast qualification passed"
}

rbq_qualify_release() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Running release qualification"

  # Phase 1: Fast qualification (tabtargets, colophons, nameplate preflight)
  rbq_qualify_fast

  # Phase 2: Shellcheck (expensive, release-only)
  buc_step "Running shellcheck"
  buq_shellcheck "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/busc_shellcheckrc" \
    "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/buq_shellcheck_results.txt"

  # Phase 3: Complete test suite (sequential)
  buc_step "Running complete test suite"
  "${ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT}/tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.complete.sh"

  buc_step "Release qualification passed"
}

# eof

1	// Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
2	//
3	// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
4	// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
5	// You may obtain a copy of the License at
6	//
7	//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
8	//
9	// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
10	// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
11	// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
12	// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
13	// limitations under the License.
14	//
15	// Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
16	//
17	// RBTDRP — pristine-lifecycle fixture for theurge release qualification
18	//
19	// Case 1 (marshal-zero-attestation) is the entry gate for `rbw-tP`. It asserts
20	// that the working tree was just zeroed by `rbw-MZ` (rblm_zero) — five
21	// violation classes are checked and ALL surfaced in one aggregated diagnostic.
22	// Failure short-circuits subsequent cases via the per-fixture fail_fast switch.
23	
24	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
25	use std::process::Command;
26	
27	use crate::case;
28	use crate::rbtdrc_crucible::rbtdrc_with_ctx;
29	use crate::rbtdre_engine::{rbtdre_Disposition, rbtdre_Fixture, rbtdre_Section, rbtdre_Verdict};
30	use crate::rbtdri_invocation::{
31	    rbtdri_invoke_global, rbtdri_invoke_imprint, rbtdri_read_burv_fact, rbtdri_Context,
32	    RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY, RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP, RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR,
33	};
34	use crate::rbtdrm_manifest::{
35	    RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ACCESS_PROBE, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LEVY, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LIST,
36	    RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_UNMAKE, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_DIVEST_DIRECTOR,
37	    RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_DIVEST_RETRIEVER, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_DIRECTOR,
38	    RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_RETRIEVER, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_MANTLE,
39	    RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE,
40	};
41	
42	/// RBRR field names referenced by the pristine-lifecycle fixture. Field
43	/// identifiers extracted as consts so the blank-field array and the
44	/// throwaway-prefix install helper share a single definition site.
45	const RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX: &str = "RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX";
46	const RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX: &str = "RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX";
47	const RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER: &str = "RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER";
48	
49	/// Site-specific RBRR fields that rblm_zero blanks. These three fields define
50	/// the depot-bound site identity (project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket
51	/// derive from CLOUD_PREFIX + DEPOT_MONIKER at kindle); an empty value is the
52	/// post-marshal-zero invariant.
53	const RBTDRP_RBRR_BLANK_FIELDS: &[&str] = &[
54	    RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX,
55	    RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX,
56	    RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER,
57	];
58	
59	/// Throwaway RBRR prefix values stamped into rbrr.env by
60	/// `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes`. Pristine-lifecycle cases that need
61	/// non-blank prefixes (depot/governor/retriever/director lifecycle) call the
62	/// helper to install these values; the marker shape distinguishes throwaway
63	/// from operator-chosen canonical values.
64	pub(crate) const RBTDRP_THROWAWAY_CLOUD_PREFIX: &str = "prlc-";
65	pub(crate) const RBTDRP_THROWAWAY_RUNTIME_PREFIX: &str = "prlr-";
66	
67	/// Family stem for the fused arc section. One depot shared across all three
68	/// arc cases; the stem keeps arc monikers separable from other fixture
69	/// families. Cases pick a numeric six-digit suffix at runtime by walking
70	/// emitted depot fact files and incrementing past the highest existing
71	/// suffix per family.
72	const RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_ARC: &str = "pristl";
73	
74	/// Static identities for the SA cycle case. The invest colophons compose
75	/// SA account names as `<role>-<identity>`; these are stable across runs
76	/// because each run uses a fresh throwaway depot project.
77	const RBTDRP_IDENTITY_RETRIEVER: &str = "pristl-ret";
78	const RBTDRP_IDENTITY_DIRECTOR: &str = "pristl-dir";
79	
80	/// Lowest valid numeric suffix for autodetected monikers. Six-digit width
81	/// keeps composed project_id length predictable: cloud_prefix(<=11) +
82	/// "d-"(2) + family(6) + suffix(6) = <=25, well under the 30-char GCP limit.
83	const RBTDRP_FAMILY_NUMERIC_FLOOR: u32 = 100000;
84	
85	/// Six-digit numeric suffix width — enforced when parsing existing fact-file
86	/// names so e.g. "pristq01" (legacy two-digit) is ignored rather than parsed
87	/// as 1 and shadowing real entries.
88	const RBTDRP_FAMILY_NUMERIC_WIDTH: usize = 6;
89	
90	/// Fact-file extension mirror of RBCC_fact_ext_depot in rbcc_Constants.sh.

44:  test ! -s "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "Working tree not clean — commit or discard before marshal-zero. See: ${z_status_temp}"

218:    buh_line "  No changes to commit — already in marshal-zero state"

221:    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"

.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json:7456:              "text": "## Character

Creates the persistent cloud infrastructure used by all downstream phases of `rbw-tP`. Runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture (BBAAB) gates and the lifecycle exercises pass. This is the "real" mantling/investing plus the "real" canonical depot levy — the state that persists for reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs.

Quota-aware: canonical depot creation is one of the daily quota draws (the other being the throwaway depot in BBAAB's depot-lifecycle case). Two GCP projects per pristine run total.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so the new colophons are available.

## Work

Add a phase to `rbw-tP` that runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes:

1. **Levy canonical depot** — `tt/rbw-dL.PayorLeviesDepot.sh`; verify GAR repository exists, worker pools provisioned. Use the canonical name (RBRR-derived), distinct from throwaway names used in BBAAB.
2. **Mantle canonical governor** — `tt/rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor.sh`; deploy governor RBRA file at the configured RBRR path.
3. **Invest canonical retriever** — `rbw-arI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to retriever's production path.
4. **Invest canonical director** — `rbw-adI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to director's production path.
5. **Roster + verify** — `rbw-arr` (retrievers) and `rbw-adr` (directors) emit per-role observation facts via type-as-extension naming. Governor presence verified implicitly via depot health (Mason SA HEAD probe surfaces in `rbw-dl` depot state per BBAAM Step 4 multi-fact reshape). Verification via fact-file presence and depot-state, not stdout-substring assertion.
6. **Active depot assertion** — `RBDC_*` derived constants resolve to the canonical depot.
7. **IAM propagation wait** — phase cannot exit until IAM propagation completes for all three production SAs; downstream phases depend on auth working.
8. **Discrete checkpoint UX** — phase exits with a clear "Canonical infrastructure ready. Re-run rbw-tP to proceed; this phase will detect existing canonical state and skip if no marshal-zero has occurred since." message.

Re-run discipline: when operator re-invokes `rbw-tP` after BBAAC has already created canonical infra, BBAAC detects existing canonical state (depot exists matching RBRR-derived name, RBRAs present) and skips the create steps. Note tension: the lifecycle fixture's case 1 gate (marshal-zero attestation) will still fail if RBRAs are present, so re-runs after canonical setup require running rbw-tP with case 1 skipped (e.g., resume from BBAAC onward via a phase-skip flag, or by single-case running individual lifecycle cases). Design call to be made during this pace.

Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists posture: skip-when-present logic must rely on cloud probe before any invest call, since blind invest atop existing cloud SA fatals. Per BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any "tear down before re-create" path must invest after divest, never invest atop existing cloud SA.

## Verification

- After phase runs: canonical depot exists, all three RBRAs present at configured paths
- `rbw-arr` and `rbw-adr` roster outputs surface all expected SAs via observation facts
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/invest/invest steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)
- Resolving the case-1-gate-vs-canonical-state re-run tension beyond a design call (full design lives in this pace)",

.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json:7634:              "text": "## Character

## Work

- Re-read RELEASE.md cold
- Apply corrections, clarifications, and restructurings captured during BBAAB–BBAAH execution but deferred from those paces
- Verify references to `rbw-tP`, the `pristine-lifecycle` fixture, and the marshal-zero signature shape match what BBAAB–BBAAH actually delivered
- Confirm cross-doc links from RELEASE.md to README.md anchors still resolve
- Confirm the README's pointer paragraph and reference-tree entry still match RELEASE.md content

## Verification

- Operator confirms the doc reads correctly cold from a release-day perspective

.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json:7645:              "text": "## Character

Sibling pace to BBAAB. Implements the simpler half of pristine-lifecycle: depot lifecycle (case 2) and governor lifecycle (case 3). No probe, no assay/canonical shuffle — these cases exercise create/list/destroy patterns without credential probing.

Depends on BBAAB landing first (fixture skeleton + case 1 must compile and register the fixture).

**Lands the shared `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` helper that BBAAK reuses** for its case 4/5 prefix-installation step.

## Locked patterns

Implementation patterns are locked in the paddock under "Implementation patterns (locked during BBAAJ)". Read those before implementing. Summary:

- **Throwaway prefixes:** edit `.rbk/rbrr.env` + `git add` + `git commit` (mimics human workflow). Env-override doesn't work — `rbrr_cli.sh` sources rbrr.env after kindle, overwriting any pre-set env.
- **Idempotent install helper:** `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` — no-op if prefixes already throwaway. One commit per fixture run.
- **Tabtarget invocation:** Tabtargets that emit identifying values (project_ids, SA emails) write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`. Handbook-style tabtargets without facts use raw `Command::new`.
- **Git shell-out:** `Command::new("git")` style, mirroring jjx/vvx. Explicit file paths. No `--no-verify` or `--amend`.

## Work

### Throwaway-prefix install helper (LANDED)

Function `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root: &Path) -> Result<(), String>` lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Drop the `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations from the helper, the two pub(crate) consts, and `rbtdrp_replace_prefix_lines` once cases 2/3 call them.

### Fact-file emission (bash + spec, BEFORE Rust cases)

- `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh`: add `RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID="rbgp_fact_depot_project_id"` and `RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL="rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email"` consts inside the existing readonly block.
- `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh::rbgp_depot_levy`: after the depot project_id is generated/captured, `buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_depot_project_id}"`.
- `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh::rbgp_governor_mantle`: after the governor SA email is known, `buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL}" "${z_governor_email}"` (variable name to be confirmed in the function body).
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`: add a "Payor Fact Files" section parallel to "Foundry Fact Files" with both new fact names as linked terms; add attribute references in the mapping section near the existing `rbf_fact_*` entries.

### Case 2: depot-lifecycle (quota-bearing — one GCP project per run)

- Call `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` (idempotent).
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dL", &["pristq01"], &[])` — levy throwaway depot.
- `rbtdri_read_burv_fact(&result, "rbgp_fact_depot_project_id")` — capture depot project_id.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dl", &[], &[])` — assert project_id substring present in stdout.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dU", &[&project_id], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")])` — soft-delete.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dl", &[], &[])` — assert project_id absent / DELETE_REQUESTED.

### Case 3: governor-lifecycle (quota-bearing — one extra GCP project per run)

`rbgp_governor_mantle` requires `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` set. Post-marshal-zero it is blank, so case 3 levies its own throwaway depot (different moniker from case 2's, e.g. `pristg01`), edits rbrr.env to set `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, runs the governor lifecycle, then unmakes its own depot.

- Call `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` (no-op if case 2 already installed).
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dL", &["pristg01"], &[])` — levy own throwaway depot.
- `rbtdri_read_burv_fact(&result, "rbgp_fact_depot_project_id")` — capture project_id.
- Edit `.rbk/rbrr.env` to set `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=<project_id>`, `git add`, `git commit`.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aM", &[], &[])` — mantle governor.
- `rbtdri_read_burv_fact(&result, "rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email")` — capture SA email.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aL", &[], &[])` — assert governor SA email substring present.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&sa_email], &[])` — forfeit.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aL", &[], &[])` — assert governor absent.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dU", &[&project_id], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")])` — unmake own depot.

### Section + manifest registration

- Add section `pristine-lifecycle-simple` to `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` (alongside the existing `pristine-lifecycle-gate` from BBAAB).
- Add colophon consts to `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`: `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LEVY` (rbw-dL), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LIST` (rbw-dl), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_UNMAKE` (rbw-dU), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_MANTLE` (rbw-aM), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_LIST_SAS` (rbw-aL), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_FORFEIT` (rbw-aF).
- Replace `Some(&[])` for `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` in `rbtdrm_required_colophons` with the six colophons above.

## Verification

- Single-case `depot-lifecycle`: creates+destroys throwaway depot; canonical depot (if present) untouched; rbrr.env commit lands on top of marshal-zero.
- Single-case `governor-lifecycle`: levies own depot, sets RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, mantles+forfeits governor, unmakes depot.
- Full fixture on marshal-zero state: case 1 passes, install commit lands once, cases 2-3 each clean up.
- Credentialed single-case verification is operator-action and is reported but not attempted by Claude.

## Out of scope

- Cases 4-5 (sibling pace BBAAK — needs probe + assay/canonical shuffle)
- Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC)

## Merge note (BBAAL parallel chat)

BBAAL changes `rbgp_depot_list` output from 4 columns to 2 columns (drops moniker + region). Case 2 asserts on **project_id substring** (column 1, format-stable) — survives BBAAL's format change either landing order. No file overlap; merge is textually clean. BBAAL has already landed (commit fe3b7595).",

fi

  buh_line "  Zero complete: ${z_rbrr}"
  buh_e

  # Auto-commit the in-tree mutations so post-zero state is captured as a single
  # commit. RBRA deletions (outside repo) are not staged.
  buc_step "Committing marshal-zero state"
  git add "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRR file"

  local z_stage=""
  for z_stage in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
    git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
  done
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    for z_stage in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
      git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
    done
  fi

  if git diff --cached --quiet; then
    buh_line "  No changes to commit — already in marshal-zero state"
  else
    git commit -m "Marshal Zero — release qualification reset" || buc_die "Marshal-zero commit failed"
    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_line "  Next: verify onboarding guide detects blank state:"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buc_success "Regime zeroed to blank template"
}

7456:              "text": "## Character

Creates the persistent cloud infrastructure used by all downstream phases of `rbw-tP`. Runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture (BBAAB) gates and the lifecycle exercises pass. This is the "real" mantling/investing plus the "real" canonical depot levy — the state that persists for reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs.

Quota-aware: canonical depot creation is one of the daily quota draws (the other being the throwaway depot in BBAAB's depot-lifecycle case). Two GCP projects per pristine run total.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so the new colophons are available.

## Work

Add a phase to `rbw-tP` that runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes:

1. **Levy canonical depot** — `tt/rbw-dL.PayorLeviesDepot.sh`; verify GAR repository exists, worker pools provisioned. Use the canonical name (RBRR-derived), distinct from throwaway names used in BBAAB.
2. **Mantle canonical governor** — `tt/rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor.sh`; deploy governor RBRA file at the configured RBRR path.
3. **Invest canonical retriever** — `rbw-arI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to retriever's production path.
4. **Invest canonical director** — `rbw-adI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to director's production path.
5. **Roster + verify** — `rbw-arr` (retrievers) and `rbw-adr` (directors) emit per-role observation facts via type-as-extension naming. Governor presence verified implicitly via depot health (Mason SA HEAD probe surfaces in `rbw-dl` depot state per BBAAM Step 4 multi-fact reshape). Verification via fact-file presence and depot-state, not stdout-substring assertion.
6. **Active depot assertion** — `RBDC_*` derived constants resolve to the canonical depot.
7. **IAM propagation wait** — phase cannot exit until IAM propagation completes for all three production SAs; downstream phases depend on auth working.
8. **Discrete checkpoint UX** — phase exits with a clear "Canonical infrastructure ready. Re-run rbw-tP to proceed; this phase will detect existing canonical state and skip if no marshal-zero has occurred since." message.

Re-run discipline: when operator re-invokes `rbw-tP` after BBAAC has already created canonical infra, BBAAC detects existing canonical state (depot exists matching RBRR-derived name, RBRAs present) and skips the create steps. Note tension: the lifecycle fixture's case 1 gate (marshal-zero attestation) will still fail if RBRAs are present, so re-runs after canonical setup require running rbw-tP with case 1 skipped (e.g., resume from BBAAC onward via a phase-skip flag, or by single-case running individual lifecycle cases). Design call to be made during this pace.

Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists posture: skip-when-present logic must rely on cloud probe before any invest call, since blind invest atop existing cloud SA fatals. Per BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any "tear down before re-create" path must invest after divest, never invest atop existing cloud SA.

## Verification

- After phase runs: canonical depot exists, all three RBRAs present at configured paths
- `rbw-arr` and `rbw-adr` roster outputs surface all expected SAs via observation facts
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/invest/invest steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)
- Resolving the case-1-gate-vs-canonical-state re-run tension beyond a design call (full design lives in this pace)",

Per BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any "tear down before re-create" path must invest after divest, never invest atop existing cloud SA.

## Verification

- After phase runs: canonical depot exists, all three RBRAs present at configured paths
- `rbw-arr` and `rbw-adr` roster outputs surface all expected SAs via observation facts
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/invest/invest steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)
- Resolving the case-1-gate-vs-canonical-state re-run tension beyond a design call (full design lives in this pace)",
7470:              "text": "## Character

Creates the persistent cloud infrastructure used by all downstream phases of `rbw-tP`. Runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture (BBAAB) gates and the lifecycle exercises pass. This is the "real" mantling/investing plus the "real" canonical depot levy — the state that persists for reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs.

Quota-aware: canonical depot creation is one of the daily quota draws (the other being the throwaway depot in BBAAB's depot-lifecycle case). Two GCP projects per pristine run total.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so the new colophons are available.

## Work

Add a phase to `rbw-tP` that runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes:

1. **Levy canonical depot** — `tt/rbw-dL.PayorLeviesDepot.sh`; verify GAR repository exists, worker pools provisioned. Use the canonical name (RBRR-derived), distinct from throwaway names used in BBAAB.
2. **Mantle canonical governor** — `tt/rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor.sh`; deploy governor RBRA file at the configured RBRR path.
3. **Invest canonical retriever** — `rbw-arI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to retriever's production path.
4. **Invest canonical director** — `rbw-adI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to director's production path.
5. **Muster + verify** — `rbw-arm` (retrievers) and `rbw-adm` (directors) emit per-role observation facts via type-as-extension naming. Governor presence verified implicitly via depot health (Mason SA HEAD probe surfaces in `rbw-dl` depot state per BBAAM Step 4 multi-fact reshape). Verification via fact-file presence and depot-state, not stdout-substring assertion.
6. **Active depot assertion** — `RBDC_*` derived constants resolve to the canonical depot.
7. **IAM propagation wait** — phase cannot exit until IAM propagation completes for all three production SAs; downstream phases depend on auth working.
8. **Discrete checkpoint UX** — phase exits with a clear "Canonical infrastructure ready. Re-run rbw-tP to proceed; this phase will detect existing canonical state and skip if no marshal-zero has occurred since." message.

Re-run discipline: when operator re-invokes `rbw-tP` after BBAAC has already created canonical infra, BBAAC detects existing canonical state (depot exists matching RBRR-derived name, RBRAs present) and skips the create steps. Note tension: the lifecycle fixture's case 1 gate (marshal-zero attestation) will still fail if RBRAs are present, so re-runs after canonical setup require running rbw-tP with case 1 skipped (e.g., resume from BBAAC onward via a phase-skip flag, or by single-case running individual lifecycle cases). Design call to be made during this pace.

Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists posture: skip-when-present logic must rely on cloud probe before any invest call, since blind invest atop existing cloud SA fatals. Per BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any "tear down before re-create" path must invest after divest, never invest atop existing cloud SA.

## Verification

- After phase runs: canonical depot exists, all three RBRAs present at configured paths
- `rbw-arm` and `rbw-adm` muster outputs surface all expected SAs via observation facts
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/invest/invest steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)
- Resolving the case-1-gate-vs-canonical-state re-run tension beyond a design call (full design lives in this pace)",

7634:              "text": "## Character

Maintenance pace — re-read RELEASE.md with fresh eyes after the rest of the heat lands and apply accumulated fixes the operator identified during heat work but did not address inline.

## Work

- Re-read RELEASE.md cold
- Apply corrections, clarifications, and restructurings captured during BBAAB–BBAAH execution but deferred from those paces
- Verify references to `rbw-tP`, the `pristine-lifecycle` fixture, and the marshal-zero signature shape match what BBAAB–BBAAH actually delivered
- Confirm cross-doc links from RELEASE.md to README.md anchors still resolve
- Confirm the README's pointer paragraph and reference-tree entry still match RELEASE.md content

## Verification

## Out of scope
